content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic boilerplate and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is literally "TODO" placeholder text (Info.plist lines 6-9), while no location feature exists anywhere in the codebase (only ScannerView.swift camera usage found).
Pierre: Apple's reviewers reject apps with placeholder or generic purpose strings because they don't tell users why the permission is needed — "TODO" will get an instant rejection, and "This app needs camera access" doesn't explain the document-scanning use case visible in ScannerView.swift. Fix the camera string to something like "SnapScan uses your camera to scan documents" and either remove the unused location key entirely (since no location code exists) or write a real justification if location is actually needed.
